---
title: "1.14 Calling Instance Methods"
subtitle: "Using Objects and Methods"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Instance Methods

### Behaviors belong to a specific object

```java
yertle.forward();
yertle.turnRight();
```

An <span class="term">instance method</span> is called on an object, not on the class name.

::: {.tight-list}
- the object before the dot is the <span class="term">receiver</span>
- the method can read or change that object's state
- the same method name can be used by many different objects
:::

## Class Methods vs. Instance Methods

### Who receives the method call?

::: {.two-col}
![Static vs instance method flow](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-14-calling-instance-methods/calling-static-vs-instance-methods.png){width="100%"}
![Method-call flowchart](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-14-calling-instance-methods/FlowChartCallingMethods.png){width="100%"}
:::

::: {.tight-list}
- class or <span class="term">static</span> method: `Math.sqrt(25)`
- instance method: `yertle.forward()`
- static methods do not depend on one object's attributes
- instance methods usually work with one object's data
:::

## Reading Instance Method Signatures

### Signatures still tell you name, types, and return type

![Student class with constructors and methods](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-14-calling-instance-methods/StudentClass.png){fig-align="center" width="64%"}

Notice the methods do <span class="mark">not</span> use the keyword `static`.

::: {.tight-list}
- the signature names the method
- it lists any parameters
- it also shows the <span class="term">return type</span>
:::

## Calling Methods Safely

### You need an actual object before the call

::: {.two-col}
![Calling methods from main or within a class](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-14-calling-instance-methods/calling-methods.png){width="100%"}
![Method parameters and arguments](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-14-calling-instance-methods/method-param-arg.png){width="100%"}
:::

```java
World habitat = new World(300, 300);
Turtle yertle = new Turtle(habitat);
yertle.forward(200);
```

If the variable is `null`, calling its method causes a <span class="term">NullPointerException</span>.

## Arguments and Overloaded Methods

### The call must match the signature

```java
yertle.forward();
yertle.forward(100);
yertle.turn(30);
```

::: {.tight-list}
- methods can be <span class="term">overloaded</span>
- arguments must match the parameter order and types
- `forward()` and `forward(100)` are different valid calls
- arguments make behavior more flexible for drawing and problem solving
:::

![Turtle UML class diagram](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-14-calling-instance-methods/turtleUMLClassDiagram.png){fig-align="center" width="42%"}

## Methods That Return Values

### Some methods produce information instead of causing visible effects

```java
int x = yertle.getXPos();
int y = yertle.getYPos();
System.out.println(x + ", " + y);
```

::: {.tight-list}
- `forward()` is a <span class="term">void method</span>: it does something
- `getXPos()` is a <span class="term">non-void method</span>: it returns a value
- getters are common instance methods for reading object state
- when a method returns a value, you need to <span class="mark">use that value</span>
:::

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- static-versus-instance call comparisons
- `null` object debugging checks
- method-call tracing with arguments and getters
- <span class="term">1.14.6 Coding Challenge: Turtle House</span>
:::

![Turtle house challenge](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-14-calling-instance-methods/house.png){fig-align="center" width="34%"}

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- distinguish class methods from instance methods
- explain why instance methods need an object receiver
- match arguments to parameters in the correct order
- explain `void` versus non-`void` methods
- recognize `NullPointerException` as calling a method on `null`
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
